across.clue: To change your ideas or behaviour so that you can deal with a new situation.
Doing something quickly because you don't have much time; rushing | in a ___
To relax and enjoy yourself, doing no work. | laze ___
A few days ago; recently (used when you don't remember or don't want to specify the exact day) | the ___ day
an amount of money that someone who no longer works because of their age or an illness etc is paid regularly either by a company they once worked for or by the government.
a needle fitted to a plastic tube used for taking blood from your body or for putting medicine or drugs into it.
Different from something else and able to be used instead of it.
one of the vehicles that are joined together to make a train.

down.clue: to fasten or join one thing to another
an amount of money that you pay into a bank account.
to travel from place to place especially on foot without a particular direction or purpose.
money that a person or institution such as a bank charges you for lending you money.
The ways in which a company encourages people to buy its products by deciding on price type of customer and advertising policy.

someone who comes to live in a country from another country.
the period of time when someone is alive.
